description: Validate flat-surface sliding friction and static-friction threshold behavior. The smoke family uses a crate, but the invariant applies to any sliding rigid body with a support contact and friction material.
physical_assumptions: A sliding body with initial horizontal velocity should decelerate under dynamic friction.
Travel distance should stay within a friction-bounded stopping envelope.
A body pushed below static-friction threshold should remain nearly stationary.
Support contact and friction labels must be present before interpreting motion.
required_assets: physics material with static and dynamic friction
verifier_rules: Support contact exists during sliding or static hold.
Sliding body with initial velocity decelerates and stops within distance range.
Static-threshold case remains below displacement and speed thresholds.
Sliding body does not accelerate or reverse without declared external force.
repair_suggestions: Declare initial velocity or applied force mode explicitly.
Bind static/dynamic friction material metadata to body and support.
Export support contact events before validating friction response.
Use a separate force-field capability if motion accelerates after launch.
